title: Pragma framework: parser
analysis: The pragma framework parser is ALREADY FULLY IMPLEMENTED on main. All four layers are complete and tested: (1) Grammar: tree-sitter-reify/grammar.js defines `pragma` rule (line 751) with `#immediate_identifier` and optional `(commaSep(pragma_arg))`, `pragma_arg` (line 758) supporting key=value and bare forms, and `_pragma_value` (line 764) restricted to number/string/bool/identifier literals. Pragma appears in `_declaration` (module level, line 47), `_member` (structure/occurrence blocks, line 346), `trait_member` (line 146), `purpose_member` (line 233), and `_constraint_def_body_item` (line 255). (2) AST: lib.rs defines Pragma (line 556), PragmaArg (line 564), PragmaValue (line 572); ParsedModule.pragmas (line 13) for module level; StructureDef.pragmas (line 42), OccurrenceDef.pragmas (line 67), TraitDecl.pragmas (line 293), PurposeDef.pragmas (line 323), ConstraintDef.pragmas (line 351) for block level. (3) Parser: ts_parser.rs implements lower_pragma() (line 781), lower_pragma_arg() (line 806), lower_pragma_value() (line 823); module-level collection in lower_source_file() (line 196); block-level collection in lower_members() (line 1038), lower_trait_members() (line 960), lower_purpose_members() (line 881), and lower_constraint_def() (line 658). (4) Tests: pragma_tests.rs has 12 passing tests covering bare pragmas, key=value args, bare value args, mixed args, multiple module pragmas, block-level pragmas in structure/occurrence/trait/purpose/constraint, scoping isolation, and boolean/number value types. All 12 tests pass. This plan contains verification-only steps confirming the existing implementation satisfies the task requirements. No new code is needed.

steps.description: Verify existing test parse_bare_module_pragma: bare pragma at module level (#optimize parses to Pragma with name='optimize' and empty args on ParsedModule.pragmas). Run `cargo test -p reify-syntax --test pragma_tests parse_bare_module_pragma` and confirm pass.

Verify existing test parse_key_value_pragma_args: pragma with key=value args (#config(level=3, name="test") parses to PragmaArg::KeyValue entries with PragmaValue::Number(3.0) and PragmaValue::String("test")). Run `cargo test -p reify-syntax --test pragma_tests parse_key_value_pragma_args` and confirm pass.

Verify existing test parse_bare_value_pragma_args: pragma with bare value args (#feature(sse2, avx) parses to PragmaArg::Bare(PragmaValue::Ident) entries). Run `cargo test -p reify-syntax --test pragma_tests parse_bare_value_pragma_args` and confirm pass.

Verify existing test parse_mixed_pragma_args: mixed pragma args (#config(debug, level=2, name="prod") parses to mix of Bare and KeyValue args with correct types). Run `cargo test -p reify-syntax --test pragma_tests parse_mixed_pragma_args` and confirm pass.

Verify existing test parse_multiple_module_pragmas: multiple module-level pragmas (#optimize + #config(level=3) both collected in ParsedModule.pragmas with correct order). Run `cargo test -p reify-syntax --test pragma_tests parse_multiple_module_pragmas` and confirm pass.

Verify existing test parse_block_pragma_in_structure: block-level pragma inside structure (structure S { #internal ... } stores pragma on StructureDef.pragmas, not module.pragmas). Run `cargo test -p reify-syntax --test pragma_tests parse_block_pragma_in_structure` and confirm pass.

Verify existing test parse_block_pragma_in_occurrence: block-level pragma inside occurrence (occurrence P { #temporal ... } stores pragma on OccurrenceDef.pragmas). Run `cargo test -p reify-syntax --test pragma_tests parse_block_pragma_in_occurrence` and confirm pass.

Verify existing test parse_block_pragma_in_trait: block-level pragma inside trait (trait R { #required ... } stores pragma on TraitDecl.pragmas). Run `cargo test -p reify-syntax --test pragma_tests parse_block_pragma_in_trait` and confirm pass.

Verify existing test parse_pragma_scoping_isolation: pragma scoping isolation (#module_level at top, #block_level inside S — each appears only in its own scope's Vec<Pragma>). Run `cargo test -p reify-syntax --test pragma_tests parse_pragma_scoping_isolation` and confirm pass.

Verify existing test parse_pragma_bool_and_number_values: boolean and number pragma values (#feature(enabled=true, count=42) parses to PragmaValue::Bool(true) and PragmaValue::Number(42.0)). Run `cargo test -p reify-syntax --test pragma_tests parse_pragma_bool_and_number_values` and confirm pass.

Verify existing test parse_block_pragma_in_purpose: block-level pragma inside purpose (purpose Optimize(...) { #solver ... } stores pragma on PurposeDef.pragmas). Run `cargo test -p reify-syntax --test pragma_tests parse_block_pragma_in_purpose` and confirm pass.

Verify existing test parse_block_pragma_in_constraint: block-level pragma inside constraint def (constraint def Positive { #validate ... } stores pragma on ConstraintDef.pragmas). Run `cargo test -p reify-syntax --test pragma_tests parse_block_pragma_in_constraint` and confirm pass.

design_decisions.decision: Pragmas use restricted compile-time values (Ident, Number, String, Bool) rather than full expressions
design_decisions.rationale: Pragmas are compiler directives resolved at compile time, so arbitrary runtime expressions would be meaningless. This contrasts with annotations (@name(expr)) which DO accept full expressions. The PragmaValue enum enforces this restriction at the type level.

design_decisions.decision: immediate_identifier token (no whitespace between # and name)
design_decisions.rationale: Prevents ambiguity — '#optimize' is a pragma, '# optimize' would be a parse error. Implemented via tree-sitter's token.immediate() which disallows whitespace between the '#' and the identifier.

design_decisions.decision: Module-level pragmas stored on ParsedModule.pragmas, block-level pragmas stored on block container types
design_decisions.rationale: Scoping by storage location — each pragma lives on its enclosing scope's AST node (StructureDef, TraitDecl, PurposeDef, ConstraintDef, OccurrenceDef). No separate 'Scope' type is needed; the container IS the scope.

design_decisions.decision: Pragmas inside guarded blocks (where ... { ... }) are silently discarded
design_decisions.rationale: GuardedGroupDecl has no pragmas field. Guarded blocks are conditional member groups within a structure, not independent compilation scopes. The enclosing structure's pragmas field is the right place for block-level compiler directives.

design_decisions.decision: Pragma args support both key=value and bare value forms in the same argument list
design_decisions.rationale: Flexibility — some pragmas need named config (#config(level=3)), others need feature flags (#feature(sse2, avx)), and mixed usage is useful (#config(debug, level=2)). The grammar uses a choice() between the two forms in pragma_arg.

design_decisions.decision: Separate collection paths for structure/occurrence (lower_members), trait (lower_trait_members), purpose (lower_purpose_members), and constraint def (inline in lower_constraint_def)
design_decisions.rationale: Each block type has a different grammar wrapper node (trait_member, purpose_member, _constraint_def_body_item, _member). The wrapper nodes require different iteration patterns, so separate collection functions are needed despite the shared lower_pragma() call.

reuse.how: Already defined and used by all block-scope containers and ParsedModule. Downstream crates (reify-compiler boundary tests) already construct these types with pragmas: vec![].
Shared lowering functions called from module-level (lower_source_file line 196) and all block-level collection functions. The same lower_pragma() is reused across 5 different collection contexts.

reuse.what: Tree-sitter grammar pragma rule and subrules
reuse.how: Pragma rule is referenced by 5 parent rules: _declaration (module), _member (structure/occurrence), trait_member, purpose_member, _constraint_def_body_item. The grammar's commaSep() helper handles comma-separated pragma_arg lists.

reuse.what: Existing test helper parse_module() and assertion patterns
reuse.how: Standard pattern across all 21 test files — wraps reify_syntax::parse() with a test ModulePath. Assertion patterns: errors.is_empty(), pragmas.len(), pattern match on PragmaArg/PragmaValue variants.
